title: Where (filter)
instruction:
`Where` filters elements matching a predicate.

Run this.
successMessage: Where returns an IEnumerable, ToList materialises it. The lambda n => n % 2 == 0 is the predicate.

title: Select (transform)
successMessage: Select maps each element to a new value. The output type can be different from the input type.

title: Chaining LINQ operations
instruction:
Chain multiple LINQ methods to build a pipeline.

Run this.
successMessage: Where filters > 3, Distinct removes duplicates, OrderBy sorts. Chains read naturally left to right.

title: OrderBy and ThenBy
instruction:
Sort with `OrderBy` and add secondary sort with `ThenBy`.

Run this.
successMessage: OrderBy sorts by Grade. ThenBy breaks ties alphabetically by Name. Bob and Carol both have 85, Bob comes first alphabetically.

title: GroupBy
instruction:
`GroupBy` groups elements by a key.

Run this.
successMessage: GroupBy returns IGrouping<K,T> objects. Each group has a Key and contains the matching elements.

title: Aggregates
instruction:
`Count`, `Sum`, `Min`, `Max`, `Average` aggregate a collection into a single value.

Run this.
successMessage: Aggregates consume the entire sequence and produce a scalar. They force evaluation — no more laziness after these.

title: First, Single, Any, All
instruction:
Predicates that return a single value or bool.

Run this.
successMessage: First throws if empty. FirstOrDefault returns default(T) if not found. Any checks if any element matches. All checks if all match.

title: Aggregate for custom reduction
instruction:
`Aggregate` is the general fold operation.

Run this.
successMessage: Aggregate combines all elements with a function. The two-argument version takes a seed value (1 for product). Same as fold/reduce in other languages.
